summary: Repo-owned hook adapters exist for Claude, Gemini, and Copilot-like surfaces, but no first-class hooks/codex adapter exists under system-spec-kit hooks.

The repo still encodes Codex as a non-hook runtime: install docs say Codex uses session_bootstrap rather than native SessionStart, runtime detection returns hookPolicy unavailable, and tests assert tool_fallback for codex-cli.

Local Codex 0.121.0 environment has codex_hooks enabled and a hooks.json wiring SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, and Stop; the Superset wrapper enables codex_hooks but its notify path is notification-oriented rather than model-context injection.

The cli-codex skill docs document instructions.md, config, profiles, and session subcommands but do not document a Codex hook contract; exact hook-term search found only an unrelated lifecycle migration prompt template.

OpenAI Codex issue evidence indicates UserPromptSubmit.additionalContext is model-visible developer-instruction context; SessionStart and Stop existed in v0.114.0; PreToolUse/PostToolUse were requested later and appear promising but need local runtime payload capture before enforcement use.

Recommended Codex strategy: add a first-class hooks/codex UserPromptSubmit adapter for advisor brief injection, use SessionStart for a short readiness preamble, keep prompt wrapping only as compatibility fallback, and update Codex hookPolicy detection from static unavailable to dynamic enabled/disabled/unavailable.
